items.headline: Israel says it has killed Iran’s intelligence minister in third assassination in two days
items.summary: Israel's Defense Forces said Wednesday that Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib had been killed in a "targeted strike" in Tehran.

items.headline: It's not just oil: Aluminum prices have surged as Iran conflict chokes supply
items.summary: The U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has upended the supply of aluminum in the Middle East, sending prices of the base metal skyrocketing.

items.headline: Wholesale prices rose 0.7% in February, much more than expected
Oil prices climb after Iran reports attacks on key oil and natural gas field
items.summary: Oil prices were popping early Wednesday morning after Iran said U.S. and Israeli airstrikes hit one of its key natural gas field, while nearby oil and petrochemical facilities also came under attack.

items.headline: It’s a ‘black swan’ moment in oil but nowhere else. The stock market is at risk of a 20% fall, say these strategists.
items.summary: Almost everything except for bonds – stocks, interest rates, credit, gold and the U.S. dollar – is having “very normal white swan types of moves.”

items.headline: Fed to still cut rates this year, even as high oil prices spark an uptick in inflation: CNBC Fed Survey

items.headline: Wholesale prices surge again and show inflation flowing through pipeline of the economy
items.summary: The cost of wholesale goods and services surged in February for the third month in a row, underscoring the challenge faced by the Federal Reserve in vanquishing inflation even before the recent spike in oil prices.

items.headline: Here’s the exact amount of money you need to make to be financially secure
items.summary: The yearly thresholds for achieving financial security range from roughly $62,700 for one-person households over age 65 to $169,300 for families with two or more adults and three or more kids.

items.headline: Mortgage refinance demand plunges 19% after interest rates shoot higher
